overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under California Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Could void the 60-month royalty liquidated damages formula, significantly reducing franchisor's leverage on termination

overrides.override_summary: CA Business and Professions Code 20000-20043 provides franchisee termination and non-renewal rights
overrides.why_it_matters: May require good cause for termination and limit franchisor's non-renewal discretion

overrides.override_summary: NY governing law provision may not be enforceable in California
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchise law may apply instead of New York law

overrides.override_summary: Virginia venue requirement may not be enforceable in California
overrides.why_it_matters: May be able to litigate in California instead of Virginia

overrides.override_summary: CA Corp Code 31512 voids waiver of Franchise Investment Law rights; Bus & Prof Code 20010 voids waiver of Franchise Relations Act rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Release signed on renewal/transfer cannot waive California statutory franchise rights

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor/affiliates from liability under Hawaii franchising laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves all Hawaii franchise law claims regardless of release language

overrides.override_summary: IL Franchise Disclosure Act governs IL licensees; venue/choice of law/jury waiver provisions do not waive IL rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois law protects franchisee's rights regarding termination (815 ILCS 705/19, 705/20) regardless of NY choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law may supersede FA termination and renewal provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional protections on termination grounds and notice requirements

overrides.override_summary: May sue in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Law; 3-year statute of limitations
overrides.why_it_matters: No need to litigate in Virginia for Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release does not release liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive Maryland franchise law protections via release

overrides.override_summary: Good cause required for termination; 30 days to cure. Cannot terminate prior to expiration except for good cause.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significant protection against arbitrary termination. All non-curable default grounds must constitute 'good cause'

overrides.override_summary: Must fairly compensate on non-renewal (FMV repurchase of inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures, furnishings) if term <5 years and post-term competition restricted or <6 months notice
overrides.why_it_matters: Since Spark has 10-15 year terms, this may not apply directly, but important if term is shortened

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require arbitration or litigation outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia/New York venue provisions are void; must litigate in Michigan

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer except for good cause (enumerated)
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's discretion on transfer approval

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination with 60 days to cure; 180 days notice of non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Much longer cure and notice periods than FA standard (10-30 days)

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages consent prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages formula may be void in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota or waive jury trial
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee retains right to litigate in Minnesota with jury trial

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve liability under MN Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law protections cannot be waived

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchises Law requires NY law govern causes of action under NY Franchise Law; NY GBL Article 33 may supersede inconsistent FA provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Since NY law already governs the FA, this primarily ensures NY franchise-specific statutes also apply

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY courts may void overly broad releases in franchise context

overrides.override_summary: All liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor has no liquidated damages remedy in North Dakota — must prove actual damages

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes NY law; FA governed by ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchise law applies regardless of NY choice of law clause

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection outside ND deleted; jury trial waiver deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Must litigate in North Dakota with jury trial preserved

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve liability under ND Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law protections cannot be waived

overrides.override_summary: Release on transfer/renewal does not apply to claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims preserved regardless of release

overrides.override_summary: Forum/venue restrictions outside RI and non-RI choice of law void for RI Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Can litigate RI Franchise Investment Act claims in Rhode Island

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel without reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act (Section 13.1-564)
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination grounds must constitute 'reasonable cause' under Virginia law

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA termination and renewal provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchise protection statute may override FA termination grounds

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights except in negotiated settlements with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: WA Act rights preserved in all releases unless specifically negotiated post-execution

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning <$100K/yr and independent contractors <$250K/yr (adjusted for inflation)
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits noncompete enforcement against hotel staff and management contractors

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed; litigation for franchise sale/WA Act violations may be brought in Washington
overrides.why_it_matters: No out-of-state dispute resolution required for WA franchise claims
